description:
You are working as the off-hours backup administrator.  
    Before today’s archiving job can start, you must record a quick
    network-reachability check of the local loopback interface.
    
    Your job:

    1. Create a directory called “backup_checks” directly inside your home directory  
       – that is, the final absolute path must be  
       “/home/user/backup_checks”.

    2. Run exactly one terminal command that
       a) executes an IPv4 ping of the loopback address 127.0.0.1,  
          limited to four packets (-c 4),  
       b) redirects *both* its stdout **and** stderr into a new log file called  
          “loopback_ping.log” that lives inside “/home/user/backup_checks”, and  
       c) **in the same command line** appends one final custom summary line.

       The summary line must be appended immediately after the normal ping
       output and it must read *exactly*:

           PING_STATUS: SUCCESS

       (all capital letters, one space after the colon, no extra
       punctuation or trailing spaces).

       • Make sure there is **one terminating newline character** 
         after that summary line.  
       • Do **not** add any other text anywhere in the file.

    3. No other files should be created or modified.

    Expected log format that the automated tests will check:

        ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
        │ 1. Standard ping banner produced by the `ping` utility     │
        │ 2. Four individual reply lines (size / ttl / time …)       │
        │ 3. Ping summary statistics (packet loss, rtt min/avg/max)  │
        │ 4. A single, final line exactly: “PING_STATUS: SUCCESS”    │
        │    (followed by a single newline)                          │
        └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

    The tests only verify that the file exists at the exact path
    “/home/user/backup_checks/loopback_ping.log” and that its **last**
    line matches the string above, so you do *not* need to worry about
    minor differences in the earlier ping output.

    Remember: everything must be done through one compound command
    (using redirection and shell operators as you see fit) once the
    directory exists.
truth:
The verifier will assert that:

    1. The directory “/home/user/backup_checks” exists and is a
       directory (0700 or any user-writable permission is fine).

    2. The file “/home/user/backup_checks/loopback_ping.log” exists,
       is non-empty, and its final line (i.e. the line returned by  
       `tail -n 1 /home/user/backup_checks/loopback_ping.log`) is the
       exact eight-word string:

           PING_STATUS: SUCCESS

       followed by a single newline (ASCII 0x0A).  
       No extra spaces, tabs, or characters are allowed after SUCCESS.

    The verifier ignores all earlier lines, so their exact wording and
    number may vary with the implementation of “ping”.
name: task_000000_fc83dfaf
